criteria.name: dashboard_layout_composition
criteria.description: The app composes as a header (title, budget cap input, capacity gauge, undo/redo), a KPI strip, a main chart region with the spend and breakdown charts, the team budget panel and anomaly list alongside, and the event table with formula box and filter chips below
criteria.reasoning: Header, KPI strip, charts, team budgets, anomaly list, event table, formula box, and filters are all visibly composed. Evidence: dashboard_layout_composition-shared.png

criteria.name: kpi_tile_anatomy
criteria.description: KPI tiles show a bold numeric value, a label, and a 16 pixel trend arrow icon, and the over-budget tile carries the 2 pixel error border and over-budget label
criteria.reasoning: Tiles show bold values, labels, trend arrows, and the over-budget tile visibly has a red 2px border and OVER BUDGET label. Evidence: kpi_tile_anatomy-shared.png

criteria.name: categorical_palette_consistent
criteria.description: Chart series use one consistent categorical palette with the same member-to-color mapping across the breakdown legend, the budget wheel, and the delta chips, and the compare overlay series is visually distinct from the primary
criteria.reasoning: Legends and budget-wheel categories use a coherent palette, while the enabled comparison series is visibly dashed and distinct from the primary line. Evidence: categorical_palette_consistent-shared.png

criteria.name: error_family_distinct
criteria.description: Anomaly markers and overage highlights share one error color family clearly distinct from the categorical series palette
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Error red is reused by the categorical meridian-pro series, so anomaly and over-budget red are not clearly distinct from the full series palette. Evidence: kpi_tile_anatomy-shared.png

criteria.name: currency_format_consistent
criteria.description: Currency renders with one consistent format — dollar sign, two decimals for totals, four for unit rates — across tiles, charts, tables, and exports
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Tiles use two decimals, but event-table costs and the formula total visibly use six decimal places. Evidence: dashboard_lower-shared.png

criteria.name: typography_hierarchy
criteria.description: Typography shows a clear hierarchy — the app title larger than panel headings, which are larger than table body and label text — consistent across views
criteria.reasoning: The title is visibly larger than panel headings, which are larger than labels and table text; computed sizes also confirm the hierarchy. Evidence: dashboard_lower-shared.png

criteria.name: spacing_rhythm
criteria.description: Gaps between the KPI strip, charts, panels, and table are visually regular with no crowded or orphaned regions
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: The desktop composition leaves a large orphaned blank region between the left chart stack and the lower scenario panel. Evidence: dashboard_lower-shared.png

criteria.name: control_state_treatments
criteria.description: Buttons, inputs, selects, sliders, and toggles show distinct default, hover, focus (visible ring), disabled, and error treatments
criteria.reasoning: Default, hover, focus-ring, disabled, checked, and error treatments were directly observed across buttons, inputs, checkbox, and over-budget controls. Evidence: kpi_tile_anatomy-shared.png

criteria.name: single_icon_set
criteria.description: One consistent icon set is used throughout the header, tiles, panel controls, and row actions
criteria.reasoning: Header actions, KPI arrows, warning marker, save/report controls, and row actions use a visually consistent outline icon style. Evidence: dashboard_lower-shared.png

criteria.name: series_rely_on_color_only
criteria.description: Chart series, anomaly, or over-budget distinctions are carried by hue alone with no accompanying label, icon, or pattern, or the same member maps to different colors in different surfaces
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Series and error states include labels, legends, dashed comparison styling, diamond markers, and warning icons; distinctions are not carried by hue alone. Evidence: categorical_palette_consistent-shared.png

criteria.name: desktop_layout_overlap
criteria.description: At 1440 pixel width any tile, chart, panel, or table content overlaps another element or is clipped by its container
criteria.reasoning: At 1440px viewport width, the major main sections have separate bounding boxes with no detected sibling overlap or clipping. Evidence: dashboard_lower-shared.png

criteria.name: visual_design_catchall
criteria.description: The app exhibits a significant, browser-observable defect in visual design that is NOT covered by any other criterion in this file. 'Significant' means it would plausibly matter to a real user, not a nitpick. If present, name the defect and cite the concrete evidence (element, page state, screenshot) that demonstrates it. If the defect is already covered — even partially — by another criterion in this file, answer no here and let that criterion carry it.
criteria.reasoning: No additional significant visual defect was observed beyond the spacing and palette issues already covered above. Evidence: dashboard_lower-shared.png

criteria.name: headings_consistent_capitalization
criteria.description: Where the app renders headings and panel titles (KPI labels, budget panel, anomaly list, report history), they use one consistent capitalization convention throughout
criteria.reasoning: Title-case headings and panel titles are consistent throughout the dashboard. Evidence: copy-quality-initial.png

criteria.name: actions_use_specific_labels
criteria.description: Where the app renders action labels, they are specific verbs — such as Recategorize, Save view, Run schedule now, Download JSON, Download CSV, Copy — not generic Submit or OK when a specific label is possible
criteria.reasoning: Actions use clear labels such as Save view, Apply, View events, Download JSON, Download CSV, Copy JSON, and Run schedule now. Evidence: copy-quality-initial.png

criteria.name: errors_name_problem_and_fix
criteria.description: Where the app renders errors — invalid capUsd, backwards range, unrecognized formula, empty schedule sections, short saved-view name — the message names the offending field and the fix, not just a rejection
criteria.reasoning: Observed validation messages identify the field and remedy: “name must be 2–60 characters” and “sections: select at least one.” Evidence: errors-and-empty-states.png

criteria.name: empty_states_explain_next_step
criteria.description: Where the app renders empty states (zero-match filters, no saved views, no snapshots), the copy explains what belongs there and how to proceed
criteria.reasoning: The saved-view empty state says “No saved views yet. Capture the current analytics scope,” giving both context and next step. Evidence: copy-quality-initial.png

criteria.name: body_copy_is_well_written
criteria.description: Where the app renders body or explanatory copy, rate how free it is of spelling and grammatical errors.
criteria.reasoning: Rendered explanatory copy is clear, grammatical, and free of noticeable spelling errors. Evidence: copy-quality-initial.png

criteria.name: terminology_is_consistent
criteria.description: The same concepts keep one name across surfaces — event, team, feature, ceiling, view — not team here and squad there
criteria.reasoning: Terms such as event, team, feature, ceiling, view, model, and cost remain consistent across the dashboard. Evidence: copy-quality-initial.png

criteria.name: numbers_dates_units_consistent
criteria.description: Currency, token counts, percentages, and dates use consistent formats and labeled units across tiles, charts, tables, and exports
criteria.reasoning: Currency uses consistent dollar formatting, token columns are labeled, percentages are explicit, and dates consistently use the displayed July 2026 format. Evidence: copy-quality-initial.png

criteria.name: success_messages_specific
criteria.description: Where the app renders confirmations, they state what happened — such as View saved or Report generated — not vague affirmations
criteria.reasoning: After saving a view, the UI showed the named “Ops view” and “Saved view active,” specifically identifying the resulting action/state. Evidence: saved-view-confirmation.png

criteria.name: placeholder_copy_visible
criteria.description: Lorem ipsum or placeholder text ('TODO', 'Lorem', 'asdf', or template variable names such as '{{title}}') is visible anywhere in the shipped UI
criteria.reasoning: No Lorem ipsum, TODO, asdf, or template-variable text was visible in the rendered UI. Evidence: copy-quality-initial.png

criteria.name: prominent_copy_has_errors
criteria.description: Spelling or grammatical errors appear in prominent copy such as panel headings or primary buttons
criteria.reasoning: No spelling or grammatical errors were observed in prominent headings or primary action labels. Evidence: copy-quality-initial.png

criteria.name: concept_names_inconsistent
criteria.description: The same concept is named inconsistently across the charts, table, and exports
criteria.reasoning: Charts, tables, saved views, and controls use consistent concept names; no conflicting naming was observed. Evidence: copy-quality-initial.png

criteria.name: copy_truncated_unreadable
criteria.description: Truncated or overflowing text renders copy unreadable
criteria.reasoning: Visible copy remained readable; no user-facing truncation or clipped text was observed. Evidence: copy-quality-initial.png

criteria.name: writing_catchall
criteria.description: The app exhibits a significant, browser-observable defect in rendered copy quality that is NOT covered by any other criterion in this file. 'Significant' means it would plausibly matter to a real user, not a nitpick. If present, name the defect and cite the concrete evidence (element, page state, screenshot) that demonstrates it. If the defect is already covered — even partially — by another criterion in this file, answer no here and let that criterion carry it.
criteria.reasoning: No significant browser-observable copy-quality defect outside the covered criteria was found. Evidence: copy-quality-initial.png
